ogTitle: South Quairading vs South Tammin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Quairading and South Tammin.
heading: South Quairading vs South Tammin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Quairading, Western Australia and South Tammin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Tammin has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to South Quairading (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Quairading or South Tammin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Quairading has a flood risk score of 56/100, while South Tammin scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Quairading scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. South Tammin scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Quairading or South Tammin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Tammin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
